title: Filling out Government forms
excerpt: I have government forms that need to be filled out on a regular basis. The data needed to complete the forms are located in my monday.com boards.  Is there a way to import a fillable pdf version of the form into monday.c…

posts.author: DCMac (Kieran Mackey)
posts.cookedHtml: I have government forms that need to be filled out on a regular basis. The data needed to complete the forms are located in my monday.com boards. Is there a way to import a fillable pdf version of the form into monday.com as perhaps a template and allow monday.com to fill out the form and then allow for an electronic signature before printing the forms that need to be mailed back to the government agency.

posts.author: shayan.moussawi (Shayan Moussawi)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey there,

There are several document generation apps that could help your use case.

Monday.com does not natively offer a native solution for this, as far as I know.

You could try to use Monday work docs and, column based placeholders but it really depends on how the form looks.

posts.author: ByronGriffin (ByronGriffin)
posts.cookedHtml: Yes, this is possible by integrating monday.com with services that allow you to work with PDF forms and electronic signatures, such as Adobe Sign or DocuSign. You can set up forms to automatically fill out using data from monday.com, sign them electronically, and then print them to send to your agency.

posts.author: Nir-Jetpack (Nir @ Jetpack Team)
posts.cookedHtml:
Yes this is possible with GetSign Marketplace app - Most popular PDF forms and electronic signature solution for monday. Takes minutes to integrate.

Let me know if this was helpful for you to achieve your goal.
